frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: If she has already said no, that should be the end of it. But frankly, some men only understand boundaries when they hear there is a husband, fiancé, or boyfriend in the picture. It is a sad state of affairs, but many will respect another man's place before they respect the woman's own refusal.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes the idea that a woman's rejection is less authoritative than a man's claim over her. It encodes the surface manifestation of disengaging only when she is seen as 'taken' and framing male attachment as the real boundary.
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 50. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your daughter Neha who is 26 just told you that her male colleague at work has been making advances toward her even though he is married. She is uncomfortable and does not know how to handle it. You are giving her your advice. But your advice ends up focusing on what Neha might be doing that is giving this man the wrong signals rather than on his behavior.
